text:
Admission Date:  2004-05-21       Discharge Date:  2008-05-27

Date of Birth:   2017-02-28       Sex:  M

Service:  CARDIOTHORACIC SURGERY

HISTORY OF THE PRESENT ILLNESS:   Mr. Sanchez is a
66-year-old gentleman who had an echocardiogram in 1983-09-24 that revealed severe aortic stenosis and biatrial
enlargement.  A repeat echocardiogram done in 27 of this
year showed an EF of 60% with moderate left atrial
enlargement, severe to critical AS, mild mitral stenosis, 1+
AR, 1+ MR.  The patient had complaints of shortness of breath
at that time.  He denied any chest pain or any associated
symptoms.  He was admitted to Baptist Emergency Hospital - Zarzamora for cardiac catheterization prior to a planned aortic
valve replacement.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1.  Emphysema.
2.  Ocular stroke.
3.  Renal cyst removal.
4.  Appendectomy.
5.  Leg surgery secondary to a motor vehicle accident as a
child.
6.  Throat polyps.
7.  Diabetes mellitus.

ALLERGIES:   The patient states allergies to aspirin and
tape.

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS:
1.  Singulair 10 mg q.d.
2.  Advair 250/50 two puffs b.i.d.
3.  Lipitor 10 mg q.d.
4.  Glucophage 1,000 mg t.i.d.
5.  Potassium chloride 10 mEq b.i.d.
6.  Sulfasalazine 500 mg b.i.d.
7.  Lasix 40 mg b.i.d.
8.  Nitroglycerin p.r.n.
9.  Tylenol with codeine q. four hours p.r.n.
10. Zyprexa 10 mg q.h.s.
11. Glipizide 10 mg b.i.d.
12. Coumadin 7.5 mg three times a week, 10 mg four times a
week.

LABORATORY/RADIOLOGIC DATA:  White count 10.3, hematocrit
36.4, platelets 261,000.  Sodium 137, potassium 4.8, chloride
104, C02 25, BUN 22, creatinine 1.2.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  The patient lives at home with his son and
his wife.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  The patient was admitted to the Novant Hospital Charlotte Orthopedic Hospital
Novant Hospital Charlotte Orthopedic Hospital James Galloway on 2004-05-21 directly to the
Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory.  The catheterization
showed severe aortic stenosis with an aortic valve area of
0.63 cm square and no coronary disease.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION IN THE CATHETERIZATION LABORATORY:
Heart rate 72, sinus, blood pressure 150/74, respiratory rate
16, alert and oriented times three.  Respiratory:  Lungs
clear to auscultation anteriorly.  Cardiovascular:  Regular
rate and rhythm with a III/VI systolic ejection murmur.
Abdomen:  Soft, nontender.  Extremities:  Right groin with
minimal staining on the dressing.  No hematoma.  No bruit.
Positive palpable distal pulses with no edema.

Following catheterization, the patient was seen by
Cardiothoracic Surgery and was anticipated to go to the
Operating Room on the following morning.  However, on the
following morning, the patient had a white blood cell count
of 18.6 and his surgery was cancelled while blood, sputum,
and urine were cultured.  Simultaneously, the patient was
seen in the Vascular Laboratory for carotid duplexes which
showed no significant stenoses in either the right or the
left carotids.  All culture data came back negative and the
patient was rescheduled for aortic valve replacement on
2003-05-29.

On 2003-05-29, the patient was brought to the Operating
Room, at which time he underwent an aortic valve replacement
with a #21 St. Jude valve.  Please see the OR report for full
details.  In summary, the patient tolerated the procedure
well and he was transferred from the Operating Room to the
Cardiothoracic Intensive Care Unit following the surgery.
The patient did well in the immediate postoperative period.
His anesthesia was reversed.  He was weaned from the
ventilator and successfully extubated on postoperative day
number one.

The patient remained hemodynamically stable off all IV
medications.  His chest tubes were removed and he was
transferred to the floor for continuing postoperative care
and cardiac rehabilitation.




                            English [**Last Name (Prefixes) 413**], M.D.  94401

Dictated By:Sandra Nichols
MEDQUIST36
